NKJV.1: For you yourselves know, brethren, that our coming to you was not in vain.
NKJV.2: But even after we had suffered before and were spitefully treated at Philippi, as you know, we were bold in our God to speak to you the gospel of God in much conflict.
NKJV.3: For our exhortation did not come from error or uncleanness, nor was it in deceit.
NKJV.4: But as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel, even so we speak, not as pleasing men, but God who tests our hearts.
NKJV.5: For neither at any time did we use flattering words, as you know, nor a cloak for covetousness— God is witness.
NKJV.6: Nor did we seek glory from men, either from you or from others, when we might have made demands as apostles of Christ.
NKJV.7: But we were gentle among you, just as a nursing mother cherishes her own children.
NKJV.8: So, affectionately longing for you, we were well pleased to impart to you not only the gospel of God, but also our own lives, because you had become dear to us.
NKJV.9: For you remember, brethren, our labor and toil; for laboring night and day, that we might not be a burden to any of you, we preached to you the gospel of God.
NKJV.10: You are witnesses, and God also, how devoutly and justly and blamelessly we behaved ourselves among you who believe;
NKJV.11: as you know how we exhorted, and comforted, and charged every one of you, as a father does his own children,
NKJV.12: that you would walk worthy of God who calls you into His own kingdom and glory.
NKJV.13: For this reason we also thank God without ceasing, because when you received the word of God which you heard from us, you welcomed it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth, the word of God, which also effectively works in you who believe.
NKJV.14: For you, brethren, became imitators of the churches of God which are in Judea in Christ Jesus. For you also suffered the same things from your own countrymen, just as they did from the Judeans,
NKJV.15: who killed both the Lord Jesus and their own prophets, and have persecuted us; and they do not please God and are contrary to all men,
NKJV.16: forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they may be saved, so as always to fill up the measure of their sins; but wrath has come upon them to the uttermost.
NKJV.17: But we, brethren, having been taken away from you for a short time in presence, not in heart, endeavored more eagerly to see your face with great desire.
NKJV.18: Therefore we wanted to come to you—even I, Paul, time and again—but Satan hindered us.
NKJV.19: For what is our hope, or joy, or crown of rejoicing? Is it not even you in the presence of our Lord Jesus Christ at His coming?
NKJV.20: For you are our glory and joy.

ESV.1: For you yourselves know, brothers,  that our coming to you was not in vain.
ESV.2: But though we had already suffered and been shamefully treated at Philippi, as you know, we had boldness in our God to declare to you the gospel of God in the midst of much conflict.
ESV.3: For our appeal does not spring from error or impurity or any attempt to deceive,
ESV.4: but just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel, so we speak, not to please man, but to please God who tests our hearts.
ESV.5: For we never came with words of flattery,  as you know, nor with a pretext for greed — God is witness.
ESV.6: Nor did we seek glory from people, whether from you or from others, though we could have made demands as apostles of Christ.
ESV.7: But we were gentle  among you, like a nursing mother taking care of her own children.
ESV.8: So, being affectionately desirous of you, we were ready to share with you not only the gospel of God but also our own selves, because you had become very dear to us.
ESV.9: For you remember, brothers, our labor and toil: we worked night and day, that we might not be a burden to any of you, while we proclaimed to you the gospel of God.
ESV.10: You are witnesses, and God also, how holy and righteous and blameless was our conduct toward you believers.
ESV.11: For you know how, like a father with his children,
ESV.12: we exhorted each one of you and encouraged you and charged you to walk in a manner worthy of God, who calls you into his own kingdom and glory.
ESV.13: And we also thank God constantly  for this, that when you received the word of God, which you heard from us, you accepted it not as the word of men  but as what it really is, the word of God, which is at work in you believers.
ESV.14: For you, brothers, became imitators of the churches of God in Christ Jesus that are in Judea. For you suffered the same things from your own countrymen as they did from the Jews,
ESV.15: who killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets, and drove us out, and displease God and oppose all mankind
ESV.16: by hindering us from speaking to the Gentiles that they might be saved — so as always to fill up the measure of their sins. But wrath has come upon them at last!
ESV.17: But since we were torn away from you, brothers, for a short time, in person not in heart, we endeavored the more eagerly and with great desire to see you face to face,
ESV.18: because we wanted to come to you — I, Paul, again and again — but Satan hindered us.
ESV.19: For what is our hope or joy or crown of boasting before our Lord Jesus at his coming? Is it not you?
ESV.20: For you are our glory and joy.

NASB.1: For you yourselves know, brethren, that our coming to you was not in vain,
NASB.2: but after we had already suffered and been mistreated in Philippi, as you know, we had the boldness in our God to speak to you the gospel of God amid much opposition.
NASB.3: For our exhortation does not [come] from error or impurity or by way of deceit;
NASB.4: but just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel, so we speak, not as pleasing men, but God who examines our hearts.
NASB.5: For we never came with flattering speech, as you know, nor with a pretext for greed-- God is witness--
NASB.6: nor did we seek glory from men, either from you or from others, even though as apostles of Christ we might have asserted our authority.
NASB.7: But we proved to be gentle among you, as a nursing [mother] tenderly cares for her own children.
NASB.8: Having so fond an affection for you, we were well-pleased to impart to you not only the gospel of God but also our own lives, because you had become very dear to us.
NASB.9: For you recall, brethren, our labor and hardship, [how] working night and day so as not to be a burden to any of you, we proclaimed to you the gospel of God.
NASB.10: You are witnesses, and [so is] God, how devoutly and uprightly and blamelessly we behaved toward you believers;
NASB.11: just as you know how we [were] exhorting and encouraging and imploring each one of you as a father [would] his own children,
NASB.12: so that you would walk in a manner worthy of the God who calls you into His own kingdom and glory.
NASB.13: For this reason we also constantly thank God that when you received the word of God which you heard from us, you accepted [it] not [as] the word of men, but [for] what it really is, the word of God, which also performs its work in you who believe.
NASB.14: For you, brethren, became imitators of the churches of God in Christ Jesus that are in Judea, for you also endured the same sufferings at the hands of your own countrymen, even as they [did] from the Jews,
NASB.15: who both killed the Lord Jesus and the prophets, and drove us out. They are not pleasing to God, but hostile to all men,
NASB.16: hindering us from speaking to the Gentiles so that they may be saved; with the result that they always fill up the measure of their sins. But wrath has come upon them to the utmost.
NASB.17: But we, brethren, having been taken away from you for a short while-- in person, not in spirit-- were all the more eager with great desire to see your face.
NASB.18: For we wanted to come to you-- I, Paul, more than once-- and [yet] Satan hindered us.
NASB.19: For who is our hope or joy or crown of exultation? Is it not even you, in the presence of our Lord Jesus at His coming?
NASB.20: For you are our glory and joy.

NLT.1: You yourselves know, dear brothers and sisters, that our visit to you was not a failure.
NLT.2: You know how badly we had been treated at Philippi just before we came to you and how much we suffered there. Yet our God gave us the courage to declare his Good News to you boldly, in spite of great opposition.
NLT.3: So you can see we were not preaching with any deceit or impure motives or trickery.
NLT.4: For we speak as messengers approved by God to be entrusted with the Good News. Our purpose is to please God, not people. He alone examines the motives of our hearts.
NLT.5: Never once did we try to win you with flattery, as you well know. And God is our witness that we were not pretending to be your friends just to get your money!
NLT.6: As for human praise, we have never sought it from you or anyone else.
NLT.7: As apostles of Christ we certainly had a right to make some demands of you, but instead we were like children among you. Or we were like a mother feeding and caring for her own children.
NLT.8: We loved you so much that we shared with you not only God’s Good News but our own lives, too.
NLT.9: Don’t you remember, dear brothers and sisters, how hard we worked among you? Night and day we toiled to earn a living so that we would not be a burden to any of you as we preached God’s Good News to you.
NLT.10: You yourselves are our witnesses — and so is God — that we were devout and honest and faultless toward all of you believers.
NLT.11: And you know that we treated each of you as a father treats his own children.
NLT.12: We pleaded with you, encouraged you, and urged you to live your lives in a way that God would consider worthy. For he called you to share in his Kingdom and glory.
NLT.13: Therefore, we never stop thanking God that when you received his message from us, you didn’t think of our words as mere human ideas. You accepted what we said as the very word of God — which, of course, it is. And this word continues to work in you who believe.
NLT.14: And then, dear brothers and sisters, you suffered persecution from your own countrymen. In this way, you imitated the believers in God’s churches in Judea who, because of their belief in Christ Jesus, suffered from their own people, the Jews.
NLT.15: For some of the Jews killed the prophets, and some even killed the Lord Jesus. Now they have persecuted us, too. They fail to please God and work against all humanity
NLT.16: as they try to keep us from preaching the Good News of salvation to the Gentiles. By doing this, they continue to pile up their sins. But the anger of God has caught up with them at last.
NLT.17: Dear brothers and sisters, after we were separated from you for a little while (though our hearts never left you), we tried very hard to come back because of our intense longing to see you again.
NLT.18: We wanted very much to come to you, and I, Paul, tried again and again, but Satan prevented us.
NLT.19: After all, what gives us hope and joy, and what will be our proud reward and crown as we stand before our Lord Jesus when he returns? It is you!
NLT.20: Yes, you are our pride and joy.

WEB.1: For you yourselves know, brothers, our visit to you wasn’t in vain,
WEB.2: but having suffered before and been shamefully treated, as you know, at Philippi, we grew bold in our God to tell you the Good News of God in much conflict.
WEB.3: For our exhortation is not of error, nor of uncleanness, nor in deception.
WEB.4: But even as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the Good News, so we speak; not as pleasing men, but God, who tests our hearts.
WEB.5: For neither were we at any time found using words of flattery, as you know, nor a cloak of covetousness (God is witness),
WEB.6: nor seeking glory from men (neither from you nor from others), when we might have claimed authority as apostles of Christ.
WEB.7: But we were gentle among you, like a nursing mother cherishes her own children.
WEB.8: Even so, affectionately longing for you, we were well pleased to impart to you, not the Good News of God only, but also our own souls, because you had become very dear to us.
WEB.9: For you remember, brothers, our labor and travail; for working night and day, that we might not burden any of you, we preached to you the Good News of God.
WEB.10: You are witnesses with God, how holy, righteously, and blamelessly we behaved ourselves toward you who believe.
WEB.11: As you know, we exhorted, comforted, and implored every one of you, as a father does his own children,
WEB.12: to the end that you should walk worthily of God, who calls you into his own Kingdom and glory.
WEB.13: For this cause we also thank God without ceasing, that, when you received from us the word of the message of God, you accepted it not as the word of men, but, as it is in truth, the word of God, which also works in you who believe.
WEB.14: For you, brothers, became imitators of the assemblies of God which are in Judea in Christ Jesus; for you also suffered the same things from your own countrymen, even as they did from the Jews;
WEB.15: who killed both the Lord Jesus and their own prophets, and drove us out, and didn’t please God, and are contrary to all men;
WEB.16: forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they may be saved; to fill up their sins always. But wrath has come on them to the uttermost.
WEB.17: But we, brothers, being bereaved of you for a short season, in presence, not in heart, tried even harder to see your face with great desire,
WEB.18: because we wanted to come to you — indeed, I, Paul, once and again — but Satan hindered us.
WEB.19: For what is our hope, or joy, or crown of rejoicing? Isn’t it even you, before our Lord Jesus  at his coming?
WEB.20: For you are our glory and our joy.

CSB17.1: For you yourselves know, brothers and sisters, that our visit <sup>ⓗ</sup> with you was not without result.
CSB17.2: On the contrary, after we had previously suffered <sup>ⓘ</sup> and were treated outrageously in Philippi, <sup>ⓙ</sup> as you know, we were emboldened by our God to speak the gospel of God to you in spite of great opposition.
CSB17.3: For our exhortation didn’t come from error or impurity <sup>ⓚ</sup> or an intent to deceive. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.4: Instead, just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted <sup>ⓜ</sup> with the gospel, <sup>ⓝ</sup> so we speak, not to please people, but rather God, <sup>ⓐ</sup> who examines our hearts. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.5: For we never used flattering speech, as you know, or had greedy <sup>ⓒ</sup> motives <sup>ⓓ</sup>  — God is our witness <sup>ⓔ</sup>  —
CSB17.6: and we didn’t seek glory <sup>ⓕ</sup> from people, either from you or from others.
CSB17.7: Although we could have been a burden as Christ’s apostles, <sup>ⓖ</sup> instead we were gentle <sup>[1]</sup> among you, as a nurse <sup>[2]</sup> nurtures her own children.
CSB17.8: We cared so much for you that we were pleased to share with you not only the gospel of God but also our own lives, because you had become dear to us.
CSB17.9: For you remember our labor and hardship, brothers and sisters. Working <sup>ⓗ</sup> night and day so that we would not burden any of you, we preached God’s gospel to you.
CSB17.10: You are witnesses, <sup>ⓘ</sup> and so is God, of how devoutly, righteously, <sup>ⓙ</sup> and blamelessly <sup>ⓚ</sup> we conducted ourselves with you believers.
CSB17.11: As you know, like a father with his own children, <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.12: we encouraged, comforted, and implored each one of you to live worthy of God, <sup>ⓜ</sup> who calls you into his own kingdom <sup>ⓝ</sup> and glory. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.13: This is why we constantly thank God, because when you received the word of God that you heard from us, you welcomed it not as a human message, but as it truly is, <sup>ⓑ</sup> the word of God, <sup>ⓒ</sup> which also works effectively in you who believe.
CSB17.14: For you, brothers and sisters, became imitators of God’s churches in Christ Jesus that are in Judea, <sup>ⓓ</sup> since you have also suffered <sup>ⓔ</sup> the same things from people of your own country, <sup>ⓕ</sup> just as they did from the Jews
CSB17.15: who killed the Lord Jesus <sup>ⓖ</sup> and the prophets and persecuted us. <sup>ⓗ</sup> They displease God and are hostile to everyone,
CSB17.16: by keeping us from speaking to the Gentiles so that they may be saved. As a result, they are constantly filling up their sins to the limit, <sup>ⓘ</sup> and wrath has overtaken them at last. <sup>[3]</sup>
CSB17.17: But as for us, brothers and sisters, after we were forced to leave you <sup>[4]</sup> for a short time (in person, not in heart <sup>ⓙ</sup>), we greatly desired and made every effort to return <sup>ⓚ</sup> and see you face to face.
CSB17.18: So we wanted to come to you — even I, Paul, time and again — but Satan <sup>ⓛ</sup> hindered us.
CSB17.19: For who is our hope or joy <sup>ⓜ</sup> or crown <sup>ⓝ</sup> of boasting <sup>ⓐ</sup> in the presence of our Lord <sup>ⓑ</sup> Jesus at his coming? <sup>ⓒ</sup> Is it not you? <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.20: Indeed you are our glory <sup>ⓔ</sup> and joy!

AMP.1: For you know, brothers and sisters, that our coming to you has not been ineffective (fruitless, in vain),
AMP.2: but after we had already suffered and been outrageously treated in Philippi, as you know, yet in [the strength of] our God we summoned the courage to proclaim boldly to you the good news of God [regarding salvation] amid great opposition.
AMP.3: For our appeal does not come from delusion or impure motives, nor [is it motivated] by deceit [our message is complete, accurate, and based on the truth—it does not change].
AMP.4: But just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel [that tells the good news of salvation through faith in Christ], so we speak, not as [if we were trying] to please people [to gain power and popularity], but to please God who examines our hearts [expecting our best].
AMP.5: For as you well know, we never came with words of flattery nor with a pretext for greed—God is our witness—
AMP.6: nor did we seek glory and honor from people, neither from you nor from anyone else, though as apostles of Christ we had the power to assert our authority.
AMP.7: But we behaved gently when we were among you, like a devoted mother tenderly caring for her own children.
AMP.8: Having such a deep affection for you, we were delighted to share with you not only God's good news but also our own lives, because you had become so very dear to us.
AMP.9: For you remember, believers, our labor and hardship. We worked night and day [practicing our trade] in order not to be a [financial] burden to any of you while we proclaimed the gospel of God to you.
AMP.10: You are witnesses, and so is God, how unworldly and just and blameless was our behavior toward you who believe [in our Lord Jesus Christ].
AMP.11: For you know how we were exhorting and encouraging and imploring each one of you just as a father does [in dealing with] his own children, [guiding you]
AMP.12: to live lives [of honor, moral courage, and personal integrity] worthy of the God who [saves you and] calls you into His own kingdom and glory.
AMP.13: And we also thank God continually for this, that when you received the word of God [concerning salvation] which you heard from us, you welcomed it not as the word of [mere] men, but as it truly is, the word of God, which is effectually at work in you who believe [exercising its inherent, supernatural power in those of faith].
AMP.14: For you, brothers and sisters, became imitators of the churches of God in Christ Jesus that are in Judea, because you too suffered the same [kind of] persecution from your own countrymen, as they did from the Jews,
AMP.15: who killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets, and harassed and drove us out; and [they] continue to be highly displeasing to God and [to show themselves] hostile to all people,
AMP.16: forbidding us from speaking to the Gentiles (non-Jews) so that they may be saved. So, as always, they fill up [to the brim] the measure of their sins [allotted to them by God]. But [God's] wrath has come upon them at last [completely and forever].
AMP.17: But since we were taken away from you, believers, for a little while—in person, but not in heart—we endeavored, with great longing to see you face to face.
AMP.18: For we wanted to come to you—I, Paul, again and again [wanted to come], but Satan hindered us.
AMP.19: For who is [the object of] our hope or joy or our victor's wreath of triumphant celebration [when we stand] in the presence of our Lord Jesus at His coming? Is it not you?
AMP.20: For you are [indeed] our glory and our joy!

MSG.1: So, friends, it's obvious that our visit to you was no waste of time.
MSG.2: We had just been given rough treatment in Philippi, as you know, but that didn't slow us down. We were sure of ourselves in God, and went right ahead and said our piece, presenting God's Message to you, defiant of the opposition.
MSG.3: God tested us thoroughly to make sure we were qualified to be trusted with this Message.
MSG.4: Be assured that when we speak to you we're not after crowd approval—only God approval. Since we've been put through that battery of tests, you're guaranteed that both we and the Message are free of error, mixed motives, or hidden agendas.
MSG.5: We never used words to butter you up. No one knows that better than you. And God knows we never used words as a smoke screen to take advantage of you.
MSG.6: Even though we had some standing as Christ's apostles, we never threw our weight around or tried to come across as important, with you or anyone else.
MSG.7: We weren't aloof with you. We took you just as you were. We were never patronizing, never condescending, but we cared for you the way a mother cares for her children.
MSG.8: We loved you dearly. Not content to just pass on the Message, we wanted to give you our hearts. And we did.
MSG.9: You remember us in those days, friends, working our fingers to the bone, up half the night, moonlighting so you wouldn't have the burden of supporting us while we proclaimed God's Message to you.
MSG.10: You saw with your own eyes how discreet and courteous we were among you, with keen sensitivity to you as fellow believers. And God knows we weren't freeloaders!
MSG.11: You experienced it all firsthand. With each of you we were like a father with his child,
MSG.12: holding your hand, whispering encouragement, showing you step-by-step how to live well before God, who called us into his own kingdom, into this delightful life.
MSG.13: And now we look back on all this and thank God, an artesian well of thanks! When you got the Message of God we preached, you didn't pass it off as just one more human opinion, but you took it to heart as God's true word to you, which it is, God himself at work in you believers!
MSG.14: Friends, do you realize that you followed in the exact footsteps of the churches of God in Judea, those who were the first to follow in the footsteps of Jesus Christ? You got the same bad treatment from your countrymen as they did from theirs, the Jews
MSG.15: who killed the Master Jesus (to say nothing of the prophets) and followed it up by running us out of town. They make themselves offensive to God and everyone else
MSG.16: by trying to keep us from telling people who've never heard of our God how to be saved. They've made a career of opposing God, and have gotten mighty good at it. But God is fed up, ready to put an end to it.
MSG.17: Do you have any idea how very homesick we became for you, dear friends? Even though it hadn't been that long and it was only our bodies that were separated from you, not our hearts, we tried our very best to get back to see you.
MSG.18: You can't imagine how much we missed you! I, Paul, tried over and over to get back, but Satan stymied us each time.
MSG.19: Who do you think we're going to be proud of when our Master Jesus appears if it's not you?

NIV.1:
Paul’s Ministry in Thessalonica
You know, brothers, that our visit to you was not a failure.
NIV.2: We had previously suffered and been insulted in Philippi, as you know, but with the help of our God we dared to tell you his gospel in spite of strong opposition.
NIV.3: For the appeal we make does not spring from error or impure motives, nor are we trying to trick you.
NIV.4: On the contrary, we speak as men approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel. We are not trying to please men but God, who tests our hearts.
NIV.5: You know we never used flattery, nor did we put on a mask to cover up greed—God is our witness.
NIV.6:
We were not looking for praise from men, not from you or anyone else.
As apostles of Christ we could have been a burden to you,
NIV.7: but we were gentle among you, like a mother caring for her little children.
NIV.8: We loved you so much that we were delighted to share with you not only the gospel of God but our lives as well, because you had become so dear to us.
NIV.9: Surely you remember, brothers, our toil and hardship; we worked night and day in order not to be a burden to anyone while we preached the gospel of God to you.
NIV.10: You are witnesses, and so is God, of how holy, righteous and blameless we were among you who believed.
NIV.11: For you know that we dealt with each of you as a father deals with his own children,
NIV.12: encouraging, comforting and urging you to live lives worthy of God, who calls you into his kingdom and glory.
NIV.13: And we also thank God continually because, when you received the word of God, which you heard from us, you accepted it not as the word of men, but as it actually is, the word of God, which is at work in you who believe.
NIV.14: For you, brothers, became imitators of God’s churches in Judea, which are in Christ Jesus: You suffered from your own countrymen the same things those churches suffered from the Jews,
NIV.15: who killed the Lord Jesus and the prophets and also drove us out. They displease God and are hostile to all men
NIV.16: in their effort to keep us from speaking to the Gentiles so that they may be saved. In this way they always heap up their sins to the limit. The wrath of God has come upon them at last.
NIV.17:
Paul’s Longing to See the Thessalonians
But, brothers, when we were torn away from you for a short time (in person, not in thought), out of our intense longing we made every effort to see you.
NIV.18: For we wanted to come to you—certainly I, Paul, did, again and again—but Satan stopped us.
NIV.19: For what is our hope, our joy, or the crown in which we will glory in the presence of our Lord Jesus when he comes? Is it not you?
NIV.20: Indeed, you are our glory and joy.

NRSVCE.1: You yourselves know, brothers and sisters, that our coming to you was not in vain,
NRSVCE.2: but though we had already suffered and been shamefully mistreated at Philippi, as you know, we had courage in our God to declare to you the gospel of God in spite of great opposition.
NRSVCE.3: For our appeal does not spring from deceit or impure motives or trickery,
NRSVCE.4: but just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the message of the gospel, even so we speak, not to please mortals, but to please God who tests our hearts.
NRSVCE.5: As you know and as God is our witness, we never came with words of flattery or with a pretext for greed;
NRSVCE.6: nor did we seek praise from mortals, whether from you or from others,
NRSVCE.7: though we might have made demands as apostles of Christ. But we were gentle among you, like a nurse tenderly caring for her own children.
NRSVCE.8: So deeply do we care for you that we are determined to share with you not only the gospel of God but also our own selves, because you have become very dear to us.
NRSVCE.9: You remember our labor and toil, brothers and sisters; we worked night and day, so that we might not burden any of you while we proclaimed to you the gospel of God.
NRSVCE.10: You are witnesses, and God also, how pure, upright, and blameless our conduct was toward you believers.
NRSVCE.11: As you know, we dealt with each one of you like a father with his children,
NRSVCE.12: urging and encouraging you and pleading that you lead a life worthy of God, who calls you into his own kingdom and glory.
NRSVCE.13: We also constantly give thanks to God for this, that when you received the word of God that you heard from us, you accepted it not as a human word but as what it really is, God’s word, which is also at work in you believers.
NRSVCE.14: For you, brothers and sisters, became imitators of the churches of God in Christ Jesus that are in Judea, for you suffered the same things from your own compatriots as they did from the Jews,
NRSVCE.15: who killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets, and drove us out; they displease God and oppose everyone
NRSVCE.16: by hindering us from speaking to the Gentiles so that they may be saved. Thus they have constantly been filling up the measure of their sins; but God’s wrath has overtaken them at last.
NRSVCE.17: As for us, brothers and sisters, when, for a short time, we were made orphans by being separated from you — in person, not in heart — we longed with great eagerness to see you face to face.
NRSVCE.18: For we wanted to come to you — certainly I, Paul, wanted to again and again — but Satan blocked our way.
NRSVCE.19: For what is our hope or joy or crown of boasting before our Lord Jesus at his coming? Is it not you?
NRSVCE.20: Yes, you are our glory and joy!

NET.1: For you yourselves know, brothers and sisters, about our coming to you — it has not proven to be purposeless.
NET.2: But although we suffered earlier and were mistreated in Philippi, as you know, we had the courage in our God to declare to you the gospel of God in spite of much opposition.
NET.3: For the appeal we make does not come from error or impurity or with deceit,
NET.4: but just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel, so we declare it, not to please people but God, who examines our hearts.
NET.5: For we never appeared with flattering speech, as you know, nor with a pretext for greed — God is our witness —
NET.6: nor to seek glory from people, either from you or from others,
NET.7: although we could have imposed our weight as apostles of Christ; instead we became little children among you. Like a nursing mother caring for her own children,
NET.8: with such affection for you we were happy to share with you not only the gospel of God but also our own lives, because you had become dear to us.
NET.9: For you recall, brothers and sisters, our toil and drudgery: By working night and day so as not to impose a burden on any of you, we preached to you the gospel of God.
NET.10: You are witnesses, and so is God, as to how holy and righteous and blameless our conduct was toward you who believe.
NET.11: As you know, we treated each one of you as a father treats his own children,
NET.12: exhorting and encouraging you and insisting that you live in a way worthy of God who calls you to his own kingdom and his glory.
NET.13: And so we too constantly thank God that when you received God’s message that you heard from us, you accepted it not as a human message, but as it truly is, God’s message, which is at work among you who believe.
NET.14: For you became imitators, brothers and sisters, of God’s churches in Christ Jesus that are in Judea, because you too suffered the same things from your own countrymen as they in fact did from the Jews,
NET.15: who killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets and persecuted us severely. They are displeasing to God and are opposed to all people,
NET.16: because they hinder us from speaking to the Gentiles so that they may be saved. Thus they constantly fill up their measure of sins, but wrath has come upon them completely.
NET.17: But when we were separated from you, brothers and sisters, for a short time (in presence, not in affection) we became all the more fervent in our great desire to see you in person.
NET.18: For we wanted to come to you (I, Paul, in fact tried again and again) but Satan thwarted us.
NET.19: For who is our hope or joy or crown to boast of before our Lord Jesus at his coming? Is it not of course you?
NET.20: For you are our glory and joy!
